title: Flatnotes
description: Self-hosted, database-less note-taking web app that stores everything as a flat folder of portable Markdown files with full-text search.
installation: Flatnotes is database-less: all notes are stored as plain Markdown files under /data (mounted on the flatnotes-data volume), with a Whoosh search index rebuilt automatically on startup. No add-on (Postgres/Redis/etc.) is required. Before deploying, rotate FLATNOTES_PASSWORD and FLATNOTES_SECRET_KEY from the template defaults: set FLATNOTES_SECRET_KEY to a long random string (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) so that auth session tokens stay valid and secure, and FLATNOTES_USERNAME/FLATNOTES_PASSWORD to your own credentials. FLATNOTES_AUTH_TYPE defaults to 'password' (single-user login); set it to 'totp' to add a TOTP second factor (also set FLATNOTES_TOTP_KEY), 'read_only' for a public read-only instance, or 'none' to disable auth entirely (only safe behind another auth proxy). The app listens on port 8080 and serves both the web UI and API. Because notes are just files on the volume, you can back them up, sync them, or edit them with any external Markdown editor. No SMTP, object store or external search engine is needed.
